Investor sentiment has shifted to extreme bearishness:

52.0% of individual investors expressed bearish sentiment over the next 6 months in the AAII survey for the week ending March 19th, the highest since May 2025.

The bearish sentiment has risen +23 percentage points since early February, the largest increase since February 2025, amid the trade war.

Meanwhile, just 30.4% of retail investors were bullish over the last week, the 3rd-lowest reading since May 2025.

Overall, bullish sentiment has declined for 8 consecutive weeks. 

As a result, the bull-bear spread is down to -21.6 percentage points, the widest gap since Mat 2025, and in-line with the 2022 bear market levels.

Is market sentiment becoming too bearish?

Misconceptions/mistakes in charting and technical analysis:

1) H&S continuation doesn't exist. H&S can only form as a top/bottom reversal

2) Analyzing long-term charts with linear scale

3) All gaps eventually get filled 

4) Technical Analysis is a forecasting tool

5) Charts are useless in extreme market conditions driven by outside forces/news events

6) Charts are short-term timing tools

7) Charts are self-fulfilling

8) If everybody is seeing that pattern then it will fail

Weekend is for the study of weekly scale charts. End of week weekly charts, end of day daily charts.

Research to be done always when markets are closed...

Charts are not helpful and create confusion if you don't clearly define your investment/trading time frame. 

A portfolio manager that rebalances his/her portfolio on a monthly/quarterly basis should not focus on daily or weekly charts.

Long-term #SILVER investors... This is the chart that you should be focusing on. 50 level was broken on the upside after decades. As long as it holds above that level, long-term investment/asset allocation theme is intact. https://t.co/YyOSoPwjge

Steep advances are euphoric. Obviously attracts its own narratives. Wakes editors up. 

Objectivity is achieved by looking at price action, not the narratives and news. 

It is not easy to pick the top or bottom of a parabolic move, but clearly better than falling for narratives at the wrong time. #SILVER/#GOLD
$ARKK https://t.co/WIA4xrzUOZ

No better time to highlight the importance of long-term averages as a trend filter. 

After breaking down the year-long average around 100K, the first rising bearish wedge found resistance at the average.

The second possible bearish wedge is having difficulty to breach the resistance area between 72K-76.5K.

Conclusion: once you establish a trend below long-term average, chances are high you are sitting on a weak price action that is exposed to more surprises on the downside. $BTCUSD

From the days when I worked in the MENA, managing funds. My CIO and CEO @LongArcNews one day stops by to reassess the market conditions after significant weakness.

He tells me Aksel, you wake up in the middle of the night, go to the kitchen and see a cockroach... You don't question, where did it come from, how did it come... You kill it first, then question. There is likely to be more of those. This is called cockroach theory in a nutshell.

So, if more and more stocks are breaching their 200-day averages... it is time to pay attention and take action.

Below 200-day average, embrace for impact. 

It can be a quick move with a V reversal or prolonged, several months.

When you are below the 200-dya average, it is best to take defensive action. $AVGO https://t.co/w7NbvbLNCM

🚨 Read this before markets open tomorrow

Thursday Mar 19: “No troops going anywhere.”

Friday Mar 20: “We’re considering winding down military efforts.”

Friday Mar 20 (same day): U.S. lifts sanctions on Iranian oil for the first time in decades.

Saturday Mar 21: Iran fires missiles at Dimona. Israel’s nuclear research site. Israel couldn’t intercept them.

Saturday Mar 21 night: “OBLITERATE their POWER PLANTS. STARTING WITH THE BIGGEST ONE FIRST.”

We’re in for another crazy week.

Turn on notifications, you don’t want to miss what’s coming.
The purist approach and resisting the temptation to jump from one method to another is the fast way for progress in developing your trading methodology.
What are you looking on a price chart?

If you haven't studied past price action and concluded that 2009-2013 type, tight and lengthy consolidations, are usually followed by strong directional moves...

How are you supposed to capitalize on the most recent Macro play on $USO which was the flipped image of 2009-2013?

Hindsight is where process is developed. It is where foundations of a trading system is built.

Naysayers wants prediction. What have you done for me lately... They are too lazy to put the work.

That is why, shut your ears, study price action. Both past and present. Enjoy.

Just ignore the naysayers who keep on complaining and complaining about how charts are useless and analysis are meaningless after the fact.

100% You need to study charts after the fact.

You need to study your winners. How a strong breakout looks like on a chart. How it does not pullback to broken pattern boundary. How high it extends, beyond the price objective.

If you don't know how best runners behave, how are you supposed to capture those and remain in position? If you don't know what a strong breakout looks like from past experience/analysis how are you supposed to resist the urge to take profits early?

As a maturing chartist and trader 80% of your time should be spent on past price behaviour. No better place to realize markets keep on repeating the same structures again again.

Just like historical events.

$BTCUSD Most probably will offer a better level for bottom fishing. Each swing overlapping the other (rising wedge structures) is usually not a trend but a counter trend move. The trend is not up. The trend is down and short sideways/up consolidations are counter-trend moves. https://t.co/cpAbJU46nw

Talk about a turn of events:

The futures-implied BASE CASE now shows the Fed pausing interest rate cuts until July 2027.

To put this into perspective, the debate in late-2025 was whether the Fed would CUT rates 3 or 4 times in 2026.

Last week, markets briefly showed a 50% chance of a Fed interest rate HIKE in 2026.

Even as President Trump's new Fed Chair is set to begin as soon as May 2026, rate cuts are being entirely priced-out.

All while one-year inflation expectations have surged to 5.0%+, now the highest since the Fed was HIKING rates.

As a result, the 10Y Note Yield is now up +40 basis points since the Iran War began.

The bond market can only weather the storm for so long.

BREAKING: President Trump is trying to figure out who "actually makes decisions" in Iran and how to get in touch with them, according to US officials.

Araghchi has been the primary intermediary in the past, but Trump advisers see him as a "fax machine" in talks, officials say.

BREAKING: President Trump's team is currently trying to find the "best point of contact" for negotiations with Iran, along with a country to mediate, per Axios.

While Oman mediated the last round, Trump is seeking Qatar as a mediator due to "mutual distrust" with the Omanis.

BREAKING: More details emerge on a potential peace deal between the US and Iran, per Axios.

Details include:

1. US officials say there could be "room to negotiate" over returning frozen assets to Iran

2. "They call it reparations. Maybe we call it return of frozen money," the official said

3. US says any deal to end the war would need to include the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, address Iran's stockpile of highly enriched uranium, and also establish a long-term agreement on Iran's nuclear program, ballistic missiles and support for proxies in the region

4. Iranian demands include a ceasefire, guarantees that the war will not resume in the future, and compensation

We expect an eventful week ahead.

BREAKING: The Trump Administration has begun "initial discussions" on what a potential peace deal with Iran might look like, per Axios.

US officials are planning the below terms:

1. No missile program for five years

2. Zero uranium enrichment

3. Decommissioning of the reactors at the Natanz, Isfahan, and Fordow nuclear facilities

4. Strict outside observation protocols around the creation and use of centrifuges and related machinery that could advance a nuclear weapons program

5. Arms control treaties with regional countries that include a missile cap no higher than 1,000

6. End of financing for Iranian proxy groups

US officials said the expectation is there will still be 2-3 additional weeks of fighting and Trump's envoys Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff are involved in the discussions.

Step #9 of our "Conflict Playbook" is near.

If you invested in the S&P 500 in 2000 and held it until 2013, you broke even.

13 years, no profit, and you lost 50% to inflation.

Think about it for a second.

Is the market setting up for a rally?

The S&P 500 is down -5% since the Iran War started, now 15 trading days into the sell-off.

In previous geopolitical conflicts, US stocks bottomed around day 15 on average, based on over 30 major geopolitical shocks since 1939.

The current sell-off is tracking almost exactly in line with the historical average and median path.

After the typical bottom, the average recovery lasted for ~40 trading days.

From there, stocks tended to move higher after reaching their pre-event levels.

Is the usual geopolitical playbook set to repeat?
